paragraphs.summary: This Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) supervisory statement (SS) sets out the PRA’s expectation as to the model risk management practices firms should adopt when using stress test models. It supp
This SS is relevant to PRA authorised banks, building societies and PRA-designated investment firms only. Credit unions are not in scope and there is currently no proposal to extend the principles to

Adopting a proportionate approach, the PRA expects the larger firms that participate in the Bank of England’s (the Bank) annual concurrent stress testing to apply the principles contained in this SS i
The expectations in this SS will take effect from Friday 1 June 2018.

sections.chapter_title: Model risk management principles for stress testing

paragraphs.summary: The PRA’s model risk management principles for stress testing are centred on four key principles:
The PRA expects firms to adopt a risk-based approach to determine the materiality of models focusing on two factors: coverage (eg size of a portfolio) and impact (eg financial, capital or risk), with

The PRA expects firms participating in the Bank’s annual concurrent stress testing to apply the principles for all stress test models. All other firms should take into account their size, nature, scal

While the concepts of materiality are articulated for certain principles, all firms should focus their validation and independent review activities commensurate with the overall use, complexity and ma

The assessment of the model risk management practices of firms participating in the Bank’s annual concurrent stress testing will form part of the Bank’s qualitative review of the annual concurrent str

In line with previous concurrent stress test qualitative review practices,2 feedback on the status of firms’ practices will be provided confidentially to each firm. In addition, the PRA will retain th

For firms not participating in the Bank’s annual concurrent stress testing the PRA will review their stress test model risk management practices as part of the supervisory review and evaluation proces

All firms applying the principles are expected to do a self-assessment of their stress test model risk management practices against the principles as part of their internal capital adequacy assessment
The four principles on model risk management for stress testing are detailed in the boxes below.

section_headings.text: SS3/18 – Model risk management principles for stress testing
Model risk management principles for stress testing
Scope of application, proportionality and model materiality
Principle 1 – Banks have an established definition of a model and maintain a model inventory
Principle 2 – Banks have implemented an effective governance framework, policies, procedures and controls to manage their model risk
Principle 3 – Banks have implemented a robust model development and implementation process and ensure appropriate use of models
Principle 4 – Banks undertake appropriate model validation and independent review activities to ensure sound model performance and greater understanding of model uncertainties
